free_text: Studies of the (d,alpha) reaction on the 10B and 11B
nuclei in the deuteron energy range (0.8 to 2.5 MeV)
Thin targets of isotopically enriched boron (96.6% 10B
and 99% 11B) obtained by evaporation on a carbon
backing. The thickness of the targets was about 15 keV
for deuteron energy 2. MeV and the thickness of the
backing was about 0.05 um.
Three silicon surface-barrier detectors on
turntable. Fourth was used as monitor, which fixed at
90 degr. (in the case 10B(d,alpha)8Be) and at 150 degr.
(in the case of 11B(d,alpha)9Be).
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
